LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HELD ON APR-10-2015, IN DEPT. 302. THE COURT ORDERED AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-COMPLAINANT NETFORTRIS ACQUISITION CO., INC.'S APPLICATION FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT FINDS BOTH ANTHONY ZABIT AND BRANDON CHANEY IN VIOLATION OF THE NON-COMPETITION CLAUSE AT PARAGRAPH 1(A)(1) OF THEIR NON-COMPETITION, NON-SOLICITATION AND CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT DATED JANUARY 24, 2014 UNDER BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE SEC. 16601, AND WOULD ENJOIN AND RESTRAIN ANTHONY ZABIT AND BRANDON FROM VIOLATING THIS CLAUSE. THE COURT DOES NOT GRANT INJUCTIVE RELIEF AS TO THE EMPLOYEE NON-SOLICITATION CLAUSE, FINDING MUCH OF THE LANGUAGE OF THAT CLAUSE TO BE INVALID AS OVERBROAD UNDER BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE SEC. 16600. THE COURT DOES NOT GRANT INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AS TO PARAGRAPH 1(A)(3) REGARDING THE CUSTOMER SOLICITATION CLAUSE. THIS CLAUSE ONLY COVERS EXISITING CUSTOMERS, AND NETFORTRIS' ARGUMENTS REFER TO POTENTIAL CUSTOMERS. THE COURT FINDS BRANDON CHANEY IN VIOLATION OF THE DISPARAGMENT CLAUSE VIA HIS FEBRUARY 26TH, 2015 E-MAIL, AND ENJOINS AND RESTRAINS BRANDON CHANEY FROM VIOLATING THIS DISPARAGEMENT CLAUSE. INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AS TO THE DISPARAGEMENT CLAUSE IS NOT GRANTED AS TO ANTHONY ZABIT. THE COURT PROVIDES COUNSEL WITH A COPY OF A PROPOSED ORDER CONTAINING THE COURT'S APPROVED ADDITIONS/DELETIONS. DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PROVIDE THE COURT WITH A CORRECTED COPY OF THE PROPOSED ORDER FOR SIGNATURE. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Apr-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. WILLIAM C. WILKA of Dudnick Detwiler Rivin & Stikker LLP, Tel No. (415) 982-1400 appearing for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Anthony Zabit and Cross-Defendant Brandon Chaney;
Attys. GARY MEYERHOFF of Dentons US LLP, Tel No. (212) 768-6740 appearing for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Netfortris Acquisition Co., Inc.

RULING - The Court required parties to appear at hearing. The matter was argued and submitted by counsel. The Court ordered as follows: Cross-Complainant Netfortris Acquisition Co., Inc.'s Application for Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is granted in part. The Court finds both Anthony Zabit and Brandon Chaney in violation of the Non-Competition Clause at Paragraph 1(a)(1) of their Non-Competition, Non-Solicitation and Confidentiality Agreement dated January 24, 2014 under Business & Professions Code Sec. 16601, and would enjoin and restrain Anthony Zabit and Brandon from violating this clause. The court does not grant injuctive relief as to the employee non-solicitation clause, finding much of the language of that clause to be invalid as overbroad under Business & Professions Code Sec. 16600. The court does not grant injunctive relief as to Paragraph 1(a)(3) regarding the customer solicitation clause. This clause only covers exisiting customers, and Netfortris' arguments refer to potential customers. The Court finds Brandon Chaney in violation of the disparagment clause via his February 26th, 2015 e-mail, and enjoins and restrains Brandon Chaney from violating this disparagement clause. Injunctive relief as to the disparagement clause is not granted as to Anthony Zabit. THE COURT PROVIDES COUNSEL WITH A COPY OF A PROPOSED ORDER CONTAINING THE COURT'S APPROVED ADDITIONS/DELETIONS. DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PROVIDE THE COURT WITH A CORRECTED COPY OF THE PROPOSED ORDER FOR SIGNATURE. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/RBU)
